metadata.title: Censys Entity Enrichment - Web Property
metadata.description: This playbook is triggered automatically when a DNS entity (domain name) is detected in a Microsoft Sentinel incident, based on an automation rule. Upon triggering, it extracts the domain name from the entity and queries the Censys API (v3) to retrieve detailed web property information including geolocation (continent, country, city, coordinates), autonomous system details (ASN, BGP prefix), WHOIS data (network, organization, contacts), services, and DNS information. The playbook constructs the web property endpoint URL using the domain name with port 443 (e.g., domain:443). The playbook retrieves the Censys API token securely from Azure Key Vault, performs the API call with retry logic (up to 3 attempts) and comprehensive error handling for HTTP status codes (200, 401, 403, 404, 422). Upon successful data retrieval, the web property data is ingested into Azure Log Analytics (Censyswebproperty_CL table) for historical analysis and reporting. The playbook then retrieves the associated incident and invokes the CensysIncidentEnrichment sub-playbook to add the enrichment data as a comment to the incident. If no incident is associated, the playbook terminates successfully after data ingestion.
prerequisites: 1. Deploy the CensysIncidentEnrichment playbook before deploying this playbook.
2. Create an Azure Key Vault and store your Censys API token as a secret named 'Censys-Access-Token'.
3. Obtain your Censys Organization ID from the Censys platform account settings.
4. Configure an automation rule in Microsoft Sentinel to trigger this playbook when DNS entities are detected.
postDeployment: 1. Grant the playbook's managed identity 'Key Vault Secrets User' role on the Azure Key Vault.
2. Authorize the Microsoft Sentinel, Key Vault, and Log Analytics Data Collector API connections.
3. Create an automation rule in Microsoft Sentinel to trigger this playbook for DNS entities.
4. Ensure the CensysIncidentEnrichment playbook is deployed and accessible.

metadata.description: Name of the Logic App (Playbook) to be deployed. Must be unique within the resource group.
Comma-separated list of ports to query for host enrichment (e.g., 80,443,22)
Your Censys Organization ID from the Censys platform account settings
Name of the deployed CensysAddIncidentComment playbook (e.g., CensysAddIncidentComment). This playbook will be called to add enrichment data as incident comments.
Name of the Azure Key Vault where the Censys API token is stored as a secret named 'Censys-Access-Token'
Azure AD Tenant ID where the Key Vault is located. Found in Azure Portal → Azure AD → Overview
